title: Retrieval features from historical analogous stints
summary: Retrieval-augmented tabular models like TabR show that nearest-neighbor context can add signal beyond global tree rules.
rationale: Retrieval-augmented tabular models like TabR show that nearest-neighbor context can add signal beyond global tree rules. Pit calls are highly local decisions, so similar historical laps/stints should provide useful empirical hazard information that fixed trees may miss.
implementation_hint: Create a compact state vector from compound, tyre age, laps remaining, race progress, position, wet flag, and new trailing-slope features. For each row, retrieve nearest historical training laps within the same broad regime and add neighbor pit-rate, average laps-to-next-pit, and distance features, or train a small retrieval specialist and blend OOF ranks.
expected_effect: Adds local analog signal, especially for sparse regimes such as wet tyres, odd degradation patterns, or unusual late-race windows.
risk: Neighbor search must be fold-isolated to avoid same-race/future-lap leakage; naive retrieval can be memory-heavy and unstable under distribution shift.
